title: Canada and Ontario wage histories
description: Hourly wages are unadjusted LFS estimates; weekly earnings are separate seasonally adjusted SEPH confirmation measures. Their levels should not be compared on one raw axis.
plainLanguageSummary: Hourly wages are unadjusted LFS estimates; weekly earnings are separate seasonally adjusted SEPH confirmation measures. Their levels should not be compared on one raw axis.
howToRead: Compare direction, turning points, and sustained divergences. Use the tooltip for exact values and observation dates; visual alignment alone does not establish causation.

calculation.formula: Published indices and explicitly labelled derived changes; no interpolation across native frequencies.
calculation.explanation: Hourly wages are unadjusted LFS estimates; weekly earnings are separate seasonally adjusted SEPH confirmation measures. Their levels should not be compared on one raw axis.
calculation.example: A value at date t uses only observations dated t or earlier unless the chart explicitly labels a visual shift.

patternsToWatch: Turning points or divergences that repeat across historical episodes
limitations: Hourly wages are unadjusted LFS estimates; weekly earnings are separate seasonally adjusted SEPH confirmation measures. Their levels should not be compared on one raw axis.

methodology.formula: Published indices and explicitly labelled derived changes; no interpolation across native frequencies.
methodology.notes: Hourly wages are unadjusted LFS estimates; weekly earnings are separate seasonally adjusted SEPH confirmation measures. Their levels should not be compared on one raw axis.
